verbGlossary.DEVELOPS: {source} creates {target}, establishes activity logic, and sets the planned construction sequence
verbGlossary.OWNS: {source} is contractually responsible for maintaining and defending {target} through all schedule updates
verbGlossary.ISSUES: {source} produces and distributes look-ahead {target}s to coordinate near-term trade contractor work
verbGlossary.DIRECTS: {source} leads the recovery {target} effort, resequencing and compressing work to regain lost time
verbGlossary.MANAGES: {source} allocates and protects {target} to maintain schedule flexibility and avoid delay exposure
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} examines {target}s for completeness, logic quality, and contract compliance before acceptance
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} requires {target} to pass the DCMA 14-point assessment or equivalent quality checks as a condition of approval
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {source} tracks {target} status and milestones on an ongoing basis to identify deviations requiring corrective action
verbGlossary.REPORTS ON: {source} compiles {target} health metrics and presents them to the owner or project leadership at regular intervals
verbGlossary.TRACKS ACROSS PROJECTS: {source} monitors {target} status across multiple projects in a portfolio to coordinate interdependencies and resources
verbGlossary.AGGREGATES: {source} rolls up detailed project {target}s into higher-level summary schedules for program-level visibility
verbGlossary.INTEGRATES DESIGN INTO: {source} incorporates design phase activities and deliverables directly into the master construction {target} under a single contract
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES: {source} determines the execution order of detail activities based on design completion, trade coordination, and constructability
verbGlossary.APPROVES: {source} formally accepts {target} submission as meeting contract requirements and authorizes proceeding
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source} establishes {target}s such as fixed completion dates, phased occupancy, or blackout periods
verbGlossary.ADJUDICATES: {source} evaluates and decides on time extension requests based on contractual entitlement and delay analysis
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source}requires specific {target}s such as noise curfews, lane closure windows, or seasonal restrictions
verbGlossary.PERFORMS: {source} executes the physical work described by detail activities in {target}
verbGlossary.MAKES PROMISES IN: {source} provides weekly work commitments through the Last Planner System, creating a reliable plan for zone-based execution
verbGlossary.STAFFS: {source} assigns labor crews to {target}d activities based on production rates and labor loading requirements
verbGlossary.REPORTS: {source} provides actual start dates, percent complete, and remaining duration for its assigned {target} activities
verbGlossary.CAUSES OR INCURS: {source} actions or inactions result in {target} delays that must be classified for entitlement and liability analysis
verbGlossary.SUBMITS: {source} formally requests additional contract time through a time extension claim supported by {target} analysis
verbGlossary.SELF-PERFORMS: {source} division executes trade work with its own forces rather than subcontracting, appearing as both {target}r and performer
verbGlossary.DRIVES THROUGH SUBMITTALS: {source}submittal review duration creates {target}s that gate procurement and fabrication activities
verbGlossary.GATES THROUGH CALCULATIONS: {source}ing organization's structural calculations and sealed drawings must be complete before dependent construction activities can start
verbGlossary.GATES THROUGH COORDINATION: MEP engineer's coordination drawings and clash resolution must be complete before mechanical, electrical, and plumbing rough-in can proceed
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES SITEWORK IN: {source} design establishes the order of earthwork, utilities, and paving activities in the site {target}
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS BY LEAD TIME: {source} material procurement lead time creates {target} that the activity cannot start before material delivery
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} manufacturing and shipping durations define the lag times required between procurement and installation activities
verbGlossary.SETS SHOP DRAWING DURATION FOR: {source} shop drawing production and approval cycle determines the lag between award and delivery for fabricated assemblies
verbGlossary.GATES ERECTION IN: {source} production completion gates the start of erection activities; steel, precast, or curtain wall cannot erect until fabrication delivers
verbGlossary.FURNISHES: {source} provides cranes, hoists, and specialty equipment whose availability windows constrain {target}d activities
verbGlossary.CLEARS: Inspection agency performs required special inspections and issues clearance, enabling {target} milestone to be marked complete
verbGlossary.VERIFIES TURNOVER AT: {source} confirms that systems meet performance requirements at turnover milestones before closeout activities can proceed
verbGlossary.IMPOSES PERMIT HOLDS ON: {source} permit review timelines and conditions create hard constraints on the construction {target}
verbGlossary.SIGNS OFF ON: {source} performs inspections and provides sign-off required before occupancy-related milestones can be achieved
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS STOP-WORK IN: Safety authority citation results in a work stoppage that creates an excusable or non-excusable delay depending on fault
verbGlossary.TIES DRAWS TO: {source}requires milestone completion as a precondition for releasing draw payments, linking cash flow to {target} progress
verbGlossary.VALIDATES DRAWS AGAINST: {source}inspector verifies that cost-loaded {target} progress matches the draw request before funding
verbGlossary.EVALUATES: {source} monitors {target} performance index and other metrics to assess contractor ability to complete on time
verbGlossary.UNDERWRITES: {source} assesses delay risk and exposure when pricing builder's risk, delay-in-startup, or liquidated damages coverage
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES CUTOVER AT: Electric utility coordinates the permanent power cutover at a project milestone, replacing temporary construction power
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES TAP AT: Water or sewer authority coordinates the utility connection at a project milestone, enabling building system testing
verbGlossary.DEFINES REQUIREMENTS FOR: {source}establishes {target} specification requirements (update frequency, software, logic rules) that govern schedule compliance
verbGlossary.AUDITS COMMISSIONING AT: Sustainability consultant audits commissioning activities at milestone checkpoints to verify LEED or green building compliance
verbGlossary.DRIVES COMPLEXITY OF: {source}count per project increases the complexity of resource leveling across competing trade {target}s
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source}coverage rate statistically tracks with overall {target} health, since uncovered scopes create schedule risk
verbGlossary.PREDICTS DESIGN-DRIVEN: {source}provides an early warning signal for potential design-driven delays in {target}
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source}rate by trade provides input to time impact analyses quantifying {target} effects of disputed changes
